untranslatable_reason: Twin Bottoms pattern requires identifying two distinct price bottoms separated by a specific rise (10%+), measuring the distance between local minima, and characterizing bottom width (narrow vs wide). This requires multi-bar pattern recognition with lookback across variable timeframes and local extrema detection, which cannot be expressed using only point-in-time indicators and simple mathematical operators. The pattern fundamentally requires: (1) detection of two local minima, (2) measurement of rise between them, (3) classification of bottom width characteristics. Standard indicators like close, volume, and moving averages cannot identify local minima or multi-bar pattern geometry.
translation_notes: The Twin Bottoms pattern is a complex candlestick/price action pattern requiring visual pattern recognition and multi-bar analysis. To properly implement this signal, a custom pattern recognition function or dedicated pattern detector would be required. The entry_long conditions above are placeholder attempts but do not capture the true pattern logic.
